title: 450. Delete Node in a BST
question:
Given a root node reference of a BST and a key, delete the node with the given key in the BST. Return the root node reference (possibly updated) of the BST.

Basically, the deletion can be divided into two stages:

  1. Search for a node to remove.
  2. If the node is found, delete the node.

Note: Time complexity should be O(height of tree).

Example: 

root = [5,3,6,2,4,null,7]
key = 3

    5
   / \
  3   6
 / \   \
2   4   7

Given key to delete is 3. So we find the node with value 3 and delete it.

One valid answer is [5,4,6,2,null,null,7], shown in the following BST.

    5
   / \
  4   6
 /     \
2       7

Another valid answer is [5,2,6,null,4,null,7].

    5
   / \
  2   6
   \   \
    4   7
code: # Definition for a binary tree node.\u000A# class TreeNode(object):\u000A#     def __init__(self, x):\u000A#         self.val \u003D x\u000A#         self.left \u003D None\u000A#         self.right \u003D None\u000A\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def deleteNode(self, root, key):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type root: TreeNode\u000A        :type key: int\u000A        :rtype: TreeNode\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        def _findMinNode(node):\u000A            node \u003D node.right\u000A            while node.left:\u000A                node \u003D node.left\u000A            return node\u000A\u000A        def _deleteNode(root, key):\u000A            if not root:\u000A                return\u000A            print root.val\u000A            if root.val \u003E key:\u000A                root.left \u003D _deleteNode(root.left, key)\u000A            elif root.val \u003C key:\u000A                root.right \u003D _deleteNode(root.right, key)\u000A            else:\u000A                if not (root.left and root.right):\u000A                    if root.left:\u000A                        return root.left\u000A                    elif root.right:\u000A                        return root.right\u000A                    else:\u000A                        return\u000A                else:\u000A                    min_node \u003D _findMinNode(root)\u000A                    root.val \u003D min_node.val\u000A                    root.right \u003D _deleteNode(root.right, min_node.val)\u000A            return root\u000A        return _deleteNode(root, key)\u000A                    \u000A
